score_evidence: The first nucleolus render was rejected because it read as a smooth purple mound and the NOR loops looked like a wrapping band.
The improved render replaces the smoother core with a dense merged cluster of dark-violet lobes plus granular/fibrillar subregion objects.
The NOR contacts were reduced to smaller arcs so they no longer read as a nucleolus membrane.
The detail render now reads closer to a bumpy dense nucleolus than the earlier smooth ellipsoid, although it still needs final surface unification.
The nucleolus stays inside the nuclear volume and does not create transport cargo, rough ER, mitosis fragments, or interaction hotspots.
minor_issues: Several larger lobes are still visually distinct instead of fully blended into one continuous granular body.
Some individual granules still read as smooth beads in the close-up instead of fully irregular biological texture.
The reference-board composition still crops the left nucleolus baseline; this is acceptable for phase gating but should be improved for presentation renders.
required_fixes: In a later polish pass, use metaball-style fusion, remeshing, or baked displacement to blend the lobe seams into a more continuous cauliflower-like mass.
Create a cleaner final presentation board after the remaining nucleus components are built.
